title: elymus trachycaulus subsp. glaucus
intro: Elymus trachycaulus subsp. glaucus, also known as blue wildrye or glaucous wildrye, is a type of grass native to North America, characterized by its bluish-gray foliage and tall, slender stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Elymus trachycaulus subsp. glaucus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support digestive health, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Elymus trachycaulus subsp. glaucus can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or by using the dried plant material in teas, tinctures, or infusions.
